title: Phansad Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Phansad Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Maharashtra

overview.short: Phansad Wildlife Sanctuary (Marathi: फणसाड वन्यजीव अभयारण्य) is a wildlife sanctuary in the Murud and Roha talukas of Raigad district, Maharashtra state, India.
overview.description: Phansad Wildlife Sanctuary (Marathi: फणसाड वन्यजीव अभयारण्य) is a wildlife sanctuary in the Murud and Roha talukas of Raigad district, Maharashtra state, India. It was created in 1986 to preserve some of the coastal woodland ecosystem of the Western Ghats and consists of 6,979 ha (17,250 acres) of forest, grasslands and wetlands. The area was once part of the hunting reserves of the princely state of Murud-Janjira.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Kuda Caves
topPlaces.description: Kuda Caves are located in the small village of Kuda, on the eastern side of the north shore of Murud-Janjira in south Konkan, India. These fifteen Buddhist caves are small, simple, and were excavated in first century BCE. The verandah of Chaitya has several reliefs of the Buddha, carved with symbols of lotus, wheel and Nagas.

topPlaces.name: Janjira State
topPlaces.description: Janjira State was a princely state in India during the British Raj. It was governed by the Siddi Khan dynasty of Habesha African descent and the state was under the suzerainty of the Bombay Presidency.

topPlaces.name: Birwadi fort
topPlaces.description: Birwadi fort (Marathi: बिरवाडी किल्ला) is hill fortress located in the village of Birwadi in Roha taluka, Raigad district, Maharashtra, India. Birwadi village is about 3 km away from Chanera village, which is on the Roha–Salav road.

topPlaces.name: Talagad
topPlaces.description: Talgad fort is located 18 km south of the Roha town on Roha-Tala-Indapur road. This fort is at an altitude of 1000 feet.

items.note: Kuda Caves are located in the small village of Kuda, on the eastern side of the north shore of Murud-Janjira in south Konkan, India. These fifteen Buddhist caves are small, simple, and were excavated in first century BCE. The verandah of Chaitya has several reliefs of the Buddha, carved with symbols of lotus, wheel and Nagas.

Janjira State was a princely state in India during the British Raj. It was governed by the Siddi Khan dynasty of Habesha African descent and the state was under the suzerainty of the Bombay Presidency.

Birwadi fort (Marathi: बिरवाडी किल्ला) is hill fortress located in the village of Birwadi in Roha taluka, Raigad district, Maharashtra, India. Birwadi village is about 3 km away from Chanera village, which is on the Roha–Salav road.
Talgad fort is located 18 km south of the Roha town on Roha-Tala-Indapur road. This fort is at an altitude of 1000 feet.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Phansad Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Phansad Wildlife Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Phansad Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport, Mumbai (85 km) and the nearest railway station is Mumbai CSMT (70 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Phansad Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Phansad Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Phansad Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Phansad Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Phansad Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kuda Caves, Janjira State, Birwadi fort, Talagad.
